channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: The 49ers agree to a 2-year contract with QB Mac Jones, AP source says
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Quarterback Mac Jones will finally join the San Francisco 49ers four years after the team nearly drafted him third overall.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Jazz fined $100,000 for not playing Markkanen
item.description: MEMPHIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA on Wednesday fined the Utah Jazz $100,000 for violating its player participation policy.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Russell Wilson expected to meet with 2 quarterback-needy teams as NFL free agency continues: reports
item.description: Russell Wilson is expected to meet with the Cleveland Browns and New York Giants, two teams needing a QB, as his NFL free agency journey continues ahead of Year 14.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL players score a shot at Hollywood through hands-on training sessions with filmmaker Deon Taylor
item.description: SANTA MONICA, Calif. (AP) — Isaac Ukwu's sights were solely set on just playing in the NFL — until two season-ending knee injuries in college served as a harsh reality of football's fragility. The setbacks reshaped Ukwu's perspective, showing him that football was part of him, but not all of him.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Riley Gaines opens up on getting a law to ban trans athletes from women's locker rooms named after her
item.description: Riley Gaines opened up on the experience of seeing a law named after her signed in West Virginia amid her mission to protect women from trans athletes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Real Madrid beats Atletico on penalties in Champions League. Arsenal, Villa, Dortmund also advance
item.description: Real Madrid beat Atletico Madrid in a Champions League penalty shootout — again — to keep its title defense alive Wednesday and advance to the quarterfinals. Defender Antonio Rüdiger scored the decisive spot-kick in a 4-2 shootout win after two Atletico players missed.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Bills to re-sign Hamlin. Buffalo also adds defensive tackle Ogunjobi to 1-year deal, AP source says
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — Safety Damar Hamlin is returning to Buffalo, and the Bills also continued restocking their defensive front by agreeing to sign tackle Larry Ogunjobi on Wednesday. The Bills announced reaching an agreement to sign Hamlin to a one-year contract.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mets' Juan Soto silences heckler by launching home run directly at him
item.description: Juan Soto got immediate revenge against a heckler Monday night by hitting a home run directly at him after he called Soto "overrated" and "overpaid.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5th anniversary of COVID cancellations brings back painful memories for teams, NCAA officials
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Robert Morris coach Andrew Toole stood next to the staging area near midcourt Tuesday night, the confetti strewn around him as the Colonials danced, mingled with fans and climbed ladders to cut down the nets.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mallory Swanson will miss Chicago Stars' NWSL season opener for personal reasons
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Stars forward Mallory Swanson will miss the team's National Women's Soccer League season opener on Friday night against the Orlando Pride for personal reasons. Coach Lorne Donaldson said Wednesday that he believed Swanson's status was week-to-week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Legendary college football coach Urban Meyer explains why he likes 14-team CFP system
item.description: Urban Meyer believes a 14-team College Football Playoff system could be good for the game of football, though it has one big caveat in it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No Donovan Mitchell? No problem for Cavaliers as they tie their team record with 15th straight win
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — The wins and accomplishments keep piling up for the NBA-leading Cleveland Cavaliers. They’ve been so frequent that players are having a hard time keeping track. “What did we do? Oh, we clinched the Central Division,” center Jarrett Allen said, chuckling. “That’s something.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN DULIK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bills agree to sign edge rusher Joey Bosa to 1-year, $12.6 million contract, AP source says
item.description: ORCHARD PARK, N.Y. (AP) — The Buffalo Bills turned to Joey Bosa to fill their pass-rush needs, agreeing with him on a one-year, $12.6 million contract, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Christopher Bell holds off Denny Hamlin at Phoenix to win third straight NASCAR Cup Series race
item.description: Bell survived a late drag race with Hamlin to become the first NASCAR Cup Series driver to win three straight races in the NextGen car, edging his teammate by 0.049 seconds to win the second-closest race in Phoenix Raceway history on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Soldier Field to host Premier League doubleheader this summer
item.description: The Premier League Summer Series is coming to Chicago this summer, featuring four Premier League clubs - AFC Bournemouth, Everton, Manchester United, and West Ham United - in a doubleheader at Soldier Field on Wednesday, July 30.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Oldest former MLB player, who once replaced Mickey Mantle on Yankees' roster, dies
item.description: Art Schallock got his shot in the major leagues after a struggling Mickey Mantle was optioned by New York in 1951.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: RSL gives up two stoppage time goals in 3-1 loss
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After jumping out to an early lead, Real Salt Lake allowed three straight goals, two in stoppage time, and loses in its first meeting with San Diego FC Saturday night at America First Field, 3-1.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Stamkos finishes 14th career hat trick in OT to give Predators 3-2 win over Blackhawks
item.description: Steven Stamkos scored at 2:39 of overtime to complete his 14th career hat trick and the Nashville Predators beat the Chicago Blackhawks 3-2 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chiefs WR Xavier Worthy released after prosecutor declines charges
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Xavier Worthy was released Saturday after a district attorney in Texas declined to pursue charges against the Kansas City Chiefs wide receiver following an arrest in which he had been accused of felony domestic violence.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO and DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A's announce 3-year Las Vegas sponsorship as club looks toward future move
item.description: The Athletics will play in Northern California for at least the next three seasons, but will also represent their future home in Las Vegas and brought four of their star players to drive home that point.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: San Francisco State University cutting men's soccer and 2 other sports
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- San Francisco State University announced plans on Thursday to cut three sports programs as part of its budget planning. The announced cuts follow a budget meeting last week that was "particularly difficult," according to an open letter posted by SFSU President Lynn Mahoney.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: White Sox reassign top 2 prospects to minor-league camp, team announces Thursday
item.description: GLENDALE, Ariz. — The White Sox reassigned two of their top prospects to minor-league camp, one a west suburban native, the team announced Thursday morning prior to its spring training game against the San Francisco Giants.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
